metadata.title: 10th Pass Double-Check: 15 Verified Gaps from 4-Agent Deep Exploration
metadata.priority: P0+P1 — guards factorial launch against FP16 crashes, NaN corruption, silent empty returns
metadata.approach: TDD: RED → GREEN → VERIFY → FIX → CHECKPOINT → CONVERGE
metadata.phasing: Phase 1 (P0 blockers) → Phase 2 (P1 credit waste prevention) → Phase 3 (P1 scientific integrity)
metadata.verification: make test-staging (must pass 6590+ tests, 0 skips, 0 failures)
metadata.prior-plan:
The 10th-pass-improvement.xml (12 tasks) was FULLY IMPLEMENTED: T1 NaN loss guard, T2 MLflow resume retry, T3 empty folds guard, T4 load_checkpoint mismatch, T5 calibration edge cases, T6 skeleton recall warning, T7 checkpoint averaging, T8 structured logging, T9 GAR image consistency, T10 GCS bucket consistency, T11 legacy env var removal, T12 topology edge cases. ALL verified in source.
This double-check plan addresses 15 GENUINELY NEW gaps found by 4 wave-1 exploration agents that the prior plan did NOT cover.

context.summary:
Four wave-1 agents (Loss/Pipeline, Flows/Orchestration, Data/Model/Ensemble, Observability/Compliance) identified 173 total gaps across the codebase. After filtering: P2 tech debt dropped, confabulated gaps dropped (verified against actual source code), duplicates merged, and already-fixed issues from the prior plan excluded.
Remaining: 15 verified, actionable gaps in 3 phases.
Key finding: The prior 12-task plan fixed the TOP blockers but missed several dtype/NaN edge cases in compound losses, silent empty-return paths in flow helpers, and missing test coverage for FP16 mixed-precision training paths.

filtering-log.dropped: Legacy POST_TRAINING_METHOD (T11) — only plural at line 1701
Topology edge cases (T12) — test file exists
Agent-2 "check_controller_cloud untested" — tests exist at test_preflight_mocked.py
Agent-2 "check_yaml_contract untested" — tests exist at test_yaml_contract_enforcement.py
Agent-2 "check_gcp_gpu_quota untested" — tests exist at test_preflight_quota.py

constraints.constraint: CLAUDE.md Rule 3: Library-first — use MONAI/PyTorch, never rewrite
CLAUDE.md Rule 16: No regex for structured data — use ast/yaml/json parsers
CLAUDE.md Rule 25: Loud failures, never silent discards
CLAUDE.md Rule 26: Greenfield — delete old conventions, no backward compat
CLAUDE.md Rule 29: Zero hardcoded parameters — read from config
Do NOT modify SkyPilot YAML env vars or accelerators without explicit instruction
All tests must pass: make test-staging (6590+ passed, 0 skipped, 0 failed)
Prior plan tasks T1-T12 are ALREADY IMPLEMENTED — do NOT re-implement

context.cold-start-instructions:
To execute this plan from a fresh session with zero context:
1. Read THIS file completely 2. Read CLAUDE.md (project rules) 3. Read the prior plan: docs/planning/v0-2_archive/original_docs/run-debug-factorial-experiment-report-10th-pass-improvement.xml (understand what was ALREADY fixed — do not duplicate) 4. Invoke /self-learning-iterative-coder 5. The skill will execute tasks in order, TDD style
Key context: The prior plan fixed 12 blockers including NaN guards, MLflow retry, empty folds, checkpoint safety, and cross-file consistency. This plan addresses 15 NEW gaps: FP16 dtype mismatch in compound losses, NaN propagation through AuxCalibCompoundLoss, silent empty returns from flow helpers, and missing test coverage for mixed-precision training paths.

phase.name: P0 Blockers — Crash Prevention
phase.description: Fix 5 issues that would crash or corrupt a factorial training job

task.title: GraphTopologyLoss FP16 dtype mismatch under autocast
task.description:
GraphTopologyLoss.forward() (loss_functions.py:540) initializes the accumulator as: loss = torch.tensor(0.0, device=logits.device, dtype=logits.dtype)
Under torch.amp.autocast (mixed_precision=True in trainer.py:415-418), logits.dtype is float16. But sub-losses (cbdice_cldice, skeleton_recall, cape) internally use softmax/operations that promote to float32. The weighted sum at lines 542-546 then adds float32 sub-loss values to a float16 accumulator, causing precision loss or overflow (FP16 max = 65504).
Fix: Force the accumulator to float32 regardless of input dtype. This matches how VesselCompoundLoss and TopologyCompoundLoss work — they never specify dtype on the accumulator, so it defaults to float32.
task.root-cause: FP16 accumulator overflow in compound loss under autocast

tdd-spec.red:
Create tests/v2/unit/losses/test_compound_loss_fp16.py:
test_graph_topology_loss_fp16_input_finite(): - logits = torch.randn(1, 2, 16, 16, 8, dtype=torch.float16, requires_grad=True) - labels = torch.randint(0, 2, (1, 1, 16, 16, 8)).float() - loss_fn = GraphTopologyLoss() - loss = loss_fn(logits.float(), labels) # sub-losses expect float32 - Also test: loss_fn(logits, labels) with float16 input directly - Assert loss.dtype == torch.float32 (accumulator must be float32) - Assert torch.isfinite(loss)
test_graph_topology_loss_fp16_gradient_finite(): - Same setup, call loss.backward() - Assert logits.grad is not None and torch.isfinite(logits.grad).all()
test_all_compound_losses_accumulator_dtype(): - Parametrize over [VesselCompoundLoss, TopologyCompoundLoss, GraphTopologyLoss, CbDiceClDiceLoss, BceDiceClDiceLoss] - For each: create float16 logits, compute loss - Assert loss.dtype is torch.float32 (never float16)
tdd-spec.green:
In loss_functions.py:540, change: loss = torch.tensor(0.0, device=logits.device, dtype=logits.dtype) to: loss = torch.tensor(0.0, device=logits.device, dtype=torch.float32)
This matches the implicit behavior of all other compound losses (VesselCompoundLoss at line 127-130, TopologyCompoundLoss at line 349-353) which never specify dtype on intermediate tensors, so they default to float32.
tdd-spec.verify: uv run pytest tests/v2/unit/losses/test_compound_loss_fp16.py -v uv run pytest tests/v2/unit/test_graph_topology_loss.py -v make test-staging

acceptance-criteria.criterion: GraphTopologyLoss accumulator is always float32 regardless of input dtype
All compound losses produce float32 output even with float16 input
All existing graph topology loss tests still pass

task.title: HL1ACELoss NaN when ALL bins are empty (extreme probabilities)
task.description:
calibration_losses.py:85-100 — HL1ACELoss._hard_binned_calibration() sets empty bins to NaN (line 188) and then uses torch.nanmean (line 90). If ALL bins are empty (e.g., extreme logits producing all-0 or all-1 probabilities after softmax), every bin is NaN, and torch.nanmean of all-NaN returns NaN.
This NaN propagates through AuxCalibCompoundLoss.forward() (line 583: result = seg_val + self.aux_calib_weight * calib_val), corrupting the total loss and poisoning optimizer state.
Fix: In HL1ACELoss.forward(), guard the nanmean result. If all bins are empty (which means the model is extremely overconfident), return 0.0 with a warning.
task.root-cause: torch.nanmean of all-NaN tensor returns NaN, propagates through loss chain

tdd-spec.green:
In calibration_losses.py HL1ACELoss.forward(), after line 92: ace_per_bc = torch.nanmean( torch.abs(mean_p_per_bin - mean_gt_per_bin), dim=-1 ) # (B, C)
Add NaN guard (before line 95): # Guard: if all bins were empty for a (batch, channel) pair, nanmean returns NaN. # Replace NaN with 0.0 — if all bins are empty, calibration is undefined. ace_per_bc = torch.where( torch.isnan(ace_per_bc), torch.zeros_like(ace_per_bc), ace_per_bc, )
tdd-spec.verify: uv run pytest tests/v2/unit/test_hl1ace_loss.py -v make test-staging

acceptance-criteria.criterion: HL1ACELoss returns finite loss for extreme logits (all bins in one bucket)
HL1ACELoss returns finite loss for uniform predictions
All existing HL1ACE tests still pass
NaN bins are replaced with 0.0, not silently ignored

task.title: AuxCalibCompoundLoss NaN isolation — seg_loss NaN poisons total
task.description:
AuxCalibCompoundLoss.forward() (loss_functions.py:579-584): seg_val = self.seg_loss(logits, labels) calib_val = self.aux_calib(logits, labels) result = seg_val + self.aux_calib_weight * calib_val
If seg_loss produces NaN (e.g., clDice degeneration in VesselCompoundLoss before the NaN guard catches it at a different compound level), the sum is NaN regardless of calib_val. The NaN guard in VesselCompoundLoss (line 120-125) only fires when VesselCompoundLoss is the TOP-LEVEL loss. When wrapped in AuxCalibCompoundLoss, the guard fires but the NaN has already been replaced with 0.0, so this specific path is safe. HOWEVER: if seg_loss is a different loss type that does NOT have a NaN guard (e.g., HausdorffDTLoss with degenerate inputs), NaN propagates.
Fix: Add NaN guard on seg_val in AuxCalibCompoundLoss.forward(). Log warning and substitute 0.0 for the NaN component, letting the other component provide gradient signal.
task.root-cause: No NaN isolation in AuxCalibCompoundLoss wrapper

tdd-spec.red:
Create tests/v2/unit/losses/test_aux_calib_nan_isolation.py:
test_aux_calib_seg_nan_returns_finite(): - Create a mock seg_loss that returns torch.tensor(float('nan')) - Wrap it in AuxCalibCompoundLoss(seg_loss=mock_seg_loss) - Call forward(logits, labels) - Assert result is finite (calib_val provides gradient signal) - Assert warning logged about NaN from seg_loss
test_aux_calib_calib_nan_returns_finite(): - Use real seg_loss (dice_ce), mock HL1ACE to return NaN - Assert result is finite (seg_val provides gradient signal)
test_aux_calib_both_nan_raises(): - Both components NaN → loss should raise ValueError (Rule 25) - No silent zero return when both components are NaN
test_aux_calib_normal_case_unchanged(): - Normal logits/labels → result equals seg + weight * calib (unchanged)
tdd-spec.verify: uv run pytest tests/v2/unit/losses/test_aux_calib_nan_isolation.py -v uv run pytest tests/v2/unit/test_compound_loss_aux_calib.py -v make test-staging

acceptance-criteria.criterion: NaN from seg_loss does not poison total loss
NaN from aux_calib does not poison total loss
Both NaN raises ValueError (Rule 25)
Normal case behavior unchanged
Warning logged when NaN component is substituted

task.title: train_one_fold_task CUDA OOM: verify diagnostic logging + re-raise
task.description:
train_flow.py:465-470 and trainer.py:465-470 — the OOM handling catches torch.cuda.OutOfMemoryError, logs diagnostics via _log_oom_diagnostics(), then re-raises. This is CORRECT behavior (Rule 25), but there are NO tests verifying the OOM diagnostic path. The existing test_oom_detection.py tests the _is_oom_error() helper but not the full _log_oom_diagnostics() flow including VRAM info extraction.
This is P0 because: if the diagnostics code itself crashes (e.g., accessing torch.cuda.memory_allocated on a CPU device), the OOM exception is swallowed and training hangs silently.
task.root-cause: OOM diagnostic path untested — could swallow the real OOM exception

tdd-spec.red:
Create tests/v2/unit/pipeline/test_trainer_oom_diagnostics.py:
test_log_oom_diagnostics_cpu_device_no_crash(): - Create minimal SegmentationTrainer with device="cpu" - Manually call _log_oom_diagnostics(RuntimeError("CUDA out of memory")) - Assert it does NOT crash (VRAM section should say "unavailable") - Assert logger.error was called with OOM diagnostic info
test_is_oom_error_detects_pytorch2_error(): - SegmentationTrainer._is_oom_error(torch.cuda.OutOfMemoryError()) → True - (only if CUDA available; otherwise test RuntimeError variant)
test_is_oom_error_detects_legacy_runtime_error(): - _is_oom_error(RuntimeError("CUDA out of memory in ...")) → True
test_is_oom_error_rejects_unrelated_runtime_error(): - _is_oom_error(RuntimeError("something else")) → False
test_train_epoch_reraises_oom(): - Mock model.forward to raise RuntimeError("CUDA out of memory") - Call trainer.train_epoch(mock_loader) - Assert RuntimeError is re-raised (NOT swallowed) - Assert _log_oom_diagnostics was called
tdd-spec.green:
Pure test file — the implementation already handles OOM correctly at trainer.py:465-470. These tests VERIFY it works as designed and guard against regressions where diagnostic code might swallow the exception.
Key: _log_oom_diagnostics (trainer.py:322-356) accesses torch.cuda.* only when self.device.type == "cuda" (line 340). Verify this guard works.

acceptance-criteria.criterion: OOM diagnostics do not crash on CPU device
OOM is always re-raised after diagnostics (never swallowed)
_is_oom_error correctly classifies both PyTorch 2.x and legacy OOM

task.title: ClassBalancedDiceLoss: all-background or single-class inputs
task.description:
ClassBalancedDiceLoss.forward() (loss_functions.py:202-237) computes inverse-frequency weights at line 220-223: weights = total_voxels / (class_counts * self.num_classes + self.smooth) weights = weights / weights.sum(dim=1, keepdim=True)
With all-background labels: class_counts for foreground = 0, so weights[foreground] = total_voxels / (0 + smooth) = very large number. This doesn't crash (smooth prevents div-by-zero) but produces extreme weight ratios (e.g., 1e5) that can destabilize early training.
With all-foreground labels: same issue for background class.
No test covers these extreme cases. The weights normalization at line 223 prevents infinity but the magnitude matters for gradient stability.
task.root-cause: Extreme weight ratios with single-class batches, untested

tdd-spec.red:
Create tests/v2/unit/losses/test_class_balanced_dice_edge_cases.py:
test_cb_dice_all_background_finite(): - logits = torch.randn(1, 2, 8, 8, 8, requires_grad=True) - labels = torch.zeros(1, 1, 8, 8, 8) # all background - loss_fn = ClassBalancedDiceLoss() - loss = loss_fn(logits, labels) - Assert torch.isfinite(loss) - loss.backward() - Assert torch.isfinite(logits.grad).all()
test_cb_dice_all_foreground_finite(): - labels = torch.ones(1, 1, 8, 8, 8) # all foreground - Same assertions
test_cb_dice_normal_case_differentiable(): - Mixed labels (random 0/1) - Verify loss is finite, gradient is non-zero
test_cb_dice_weight_ratio_bounded(): - With all-background labels, verify weights normalization produces values where max(weights) / min(weights) < 1e6 - This is a sanity check, not a hard constraint
tdd-spec.green: Pure test file — verifying existing behavior is safe. The smooth=1e-5 parameter (line 200) and the weights normalization (line 223) should keep things finite. If any test reveals a bug, add a clamp: weights = weights.clamp(max=1e4) # Prevent extreme ratios before the normalization at line 223.

acceptance-criteria.criterion: ClassBalancedDiceLoss is finite with all-background labels
ClassBalancedDiceLoss is finite with all-foreground labels
Gradient is finite in all single-class cases
Weight ratios do not exceed 1e6 after normalization

phase.name: P1 Credit Waste Prevention
phase.description: Fix silent empty returns that waste cloud credits in production factorial runs

task.title: resolve_checkpoint_paths_from_contract: loud failure on empty result
task.description:
post_training_flow.py:49-113 — resolve_checkpoint_paths_from_contract() returns an empty list when: (a) parent_run_id is None (line 74), (b) no fold checkpoints found (all dirs missing), (c) FlowContract.find_fold_checkpoints returns empty.
The caller at line 277 receives [] and post-training silently skips ALL post-training methods (checkpoint averaging, SWAG, calibration). This wastes the entire post-training GPU job cost with zero output.
Fix: When the result is empty AND parent_run_id was provided (meaning we EXPECTED checkpoints), raise ValueError. When parent_run_id is None, log a warning and return [] (that is the intended "no upstream" path).
task.root-cause: Empty checkpoint list silently skips all post-training (Rule 25 violation)

tdd-spec.red:
Create tests/v2/unit/orchestration/test_resolve_checkpoint_paths.py:
test_resolve_checkpoint_none_parent_returns_empty(): - result = resolve_checkpoint_paths_from_contract(parent_run_id=None, tracking_uri="...") - Assert result == [] # This is correct — no upstream
test_resolve_checkpoint_missing_dirs_raises(): - Mock FlowContract.find_fold_checkpoints to return infos with non-existent dirs - result = resolve_checkpoint_paths_from_contract(parent_run_id="abc123", ...) - Assert raises ValueError with message about missing checkpoints (or returns empty and logs error — either way, NOT silent)
test_resolve_checkpoint_valid_paths(): - Mock FlowContract + create actual checkpoint files in tmp_path - Assert result contains the correct paths
tdd-spec.green:
In post_training_flow.py resolve_checkpoint_paths_from_contract(), after the for-loop (before return at line 113):
if parent_run_id is not None and not checkpoint_paths: logger.error( "No checkpoint files found for parent run %s. " "Check that training completed and checkpoints are volume-mounted. " "Fold dirs checked: %s", parent_run_id, [info["checkpoint_dir"] for info in fold_infos], ) raise ValueError( f"resolve_checkpoint_paths_from_contract found 0 checkpoints " f"for parent_run_id={parent_run_id}. " "Post-training cannot proceed without checkpoints." )

acceptance-criteria.criterion: Empty result with parent_run_id raises ValueError (not silent skip)
None parent_run_id still returns [] (intended no-upstream path)
Error message is actionable (mentions volume mounting)

task.title: _discover_runs: empty experiment or zero matching runs must warn loudly
task.description:
analysis_flow.py:152-181 — _discover_runs() delegates to EnsembleBuilder.discover_training_runs(). The builder at builder.py:210-218 returns [] when experiment doesn't exist (with a warning). But the analysis flow at line 430-437 just logs the count and continues. If zero runs are found, the entire analysis flow does no evaluation work — wasting the GPU job.
Fix: Add a loud failure in the analysis flow task (not in the builder, which is correctly a query). The flow task should raise when zero runs found and the user explicitly asked for analysis.
task.root-cause: Zero training runs → silent no-op analysis flow (Rule 25 violation)

tdd-spec.red:
Create tests/v2/unit/orchestration/test_analysis_flow_empty_runs.py:
test_discover_runs_missing_experiment_returns_empty(): - Mock MlflowClient.get_experiment_by_name to return None - result = _discover_runs(eval_config, model_config_dict) - Assert result == [] and warning was logged
test_discover_runs_no_matching_runs_returns_empty(): - Mock client.search_runs to return [] - result = _discover_runs(eval_config, model_config_dict) - Assert result == []
test_discover_runs_with_matching_runs(): - Mock client.search_runs to return runs with loss_function tag - result = _discover_runs(eval_config, model_config_dict) - Assert len(result) > 0
test_builder_experiment_not_found_warns(caplog): - Set up EnsembleBuilder with non-existent experiment - Mock MLflow client to return None for get_experiment_by_name - Call discover_training_runs() - Assert "not found" in caplog warning messages
tdd-spec.green:
Pure test file — the builder correctly returns [] with a warning. The analysis flow already logs the count. These tests verify the contract: empty result is a valid return (not a crash), but the WARNING is logged so monitoring can detect it.
NOTE: The flow-level guard (raising when zero runs) should be at the analysis_flow entry point, not in _discover_runs. If the tests reveal the warning is missing, add it to builder.py:213-218.

acceptance-criteria.criterion: Missing experiment name produces a logged warning (not silent)
Zero matching runs produces a logged warning
Builder returns [] cleanly (not crash) for missing experiments

task.title: Cost estimate edge cases: zero epochs, zero rate, negative time
task.description:
infrastructure_timing.py:240-282 — estimate_cost_from_first_epoch() and train_flow.py:59-104 — log_epoch0_cost_estimate() have guards for max_epochs <= 1 and training_time_seconds <= 0. But no tests verify: (a) epoch_seconds=0 (instant epoch — possible with tiny debug data), (b) hourly_rate_usd=0 (local runs — should produce $0 cost, not NaN), (c) num_folds=0 (should not crash, produces $0).
These are tested implicitly by production runs but never explicitly in unit tests. If any edge case produces NaN, mlflow.log_metrics() at train_flow.py:97 logs NaN to MLflow, which corrupts the experiment tracking.
task.root-cause: Cost estimate edge cases untested — NaN could corrupt MLflow metrics

tdd-spec.red:
Create tests/v2/unit/observability/test_cost_estimate_edge_cases.py:
test_cost_estimate_zero_epoch_seconds(): - result = estimate_cost_from_first_epoch(epoch_seconds=0, max_epochs=100, num_folds=3, hourly_rate_usd=0.55) - Assert all values are finite (should be $0)
test_cost_estimate_zero_hourly_rate(): - result = estimate_cost_from_first_epoch(epoch_seconds=120, max_epochs=100, num_folds=3, hourly_rate_usd=0.0) - Assert est/total_cost == 0.0
test_cost_estimate_zero_folds(): - result = estimate_cost_from_first_epoch(epoch_seconds=120, max_epochs=100, num_folds=0, hourly_rate_usd=0.55) - Assert all values are finite (0 folds * anything = setup cost only)
test_cost_estimate_normal_case(): - result = estimate_cost_from_first_epoch(epoch_seconds=60, max_epochs=100, num_folds=3, hourly_rate_usd=0.55) - Assert est/total_cost > 0 - Assert est/total_hours > 0 - Assert all values are finite
test_log_epoch0_cost_skipped_single_epoch(): - Mock mlflow.log_metrics - log_epoch0_cost_estimate(training_time_seconds=60, max_epochs=1, num_folds=3, hourly_rate_usd=0.55) - Assert mlflow.log_metrics was NOT called (guard at line 82)
test_get_hourly_rate_invalid_env(monkeypatch): - monkeypatch.setenv("INSTANCE_HOURLY_USD", "not-a-number") - rate = get_hourly_rate_usd() - Assert rate == 0.0 and warning was logged
tdd-spec.green: Pure test file — verifying existing behavior. The implementation at infrastructure_timing.py:240-282 should handle all cases correctly via multiplication (0 * anything = 0). If any test fails, add guards.

acceptance-criteria.criterion: All edge cases produce finite values (never NaN/Inf)
Zero hourly rate produces $0 cost
Single-epoch guard skips cost logging
Invalid INSTANCE_HOURLY_USD falls back to 0.0 with warning

task.title: CAPELoss: all-background GT returns zero with gradient path
task.description:
cape.py:90-91 — when valid_count == 0 (all GT samples have empty skeleton), CAPELoss returns torch.tensor(0.0, device=logits.device, requires_grad=True). This is a new tensor NOT connected to logits, so backward() produces zero gradient. This is CORRECT semantics (no path to enforce = no penalty), but it means GraphTopologyLoss with all-background data gets 0 gradient from CAPE AND from skeleton_recall (which has the same guard). Only cbdice_cldice provides gradient signal.
The risk: if cbdice_cldice ALSO degenerates (NaN guard fires → 0.0), the entire GraphTopologyLoss returns 0 with zero gradient, and training makes no progress. No test covers this compound all-background-all-degenerate scenario.
task.root-cause: Compound loss all-background: all sub-losses degenerate to zero gradient

tdd-spec.red:
Create tests/v2/unit/losses/test_compound_loss_all_background.py:
test_graph_topology_all_background_finite(): - logits = torch.randn(1, 2, 16, 16, 8, requires_grad=True) - labels = torch.zeros(1, 1, 16, 16, 8) # ALL background - loss_fn = GraphTopologyLoss() - loss = loss_fn(logits, labels) - Assert torch.isfinite(loss)
test_graph_topology_all_background_gradient_exists(): - Same setup, call loss.backward() - Assert logits.grad is not None - NOTE: gradient may be all-zero if ALL sub-losses degenerate. That is acceptable (it is semantically correct — no topology to preserve in all-background data). The test verifies no CRASH, not non-zero gradient.
test_vessel_compound_all_background_finite(): - VesselCompoundLoss with all-background labels - Assert finite loss (NaN guard should fire)
test_cbdice_cldice_all_background_finite(): - CbDiceClDiceLoss with all-background labels - Assert finite loss
test_topology_compound_all_background_finite(): - TopologyCompoundLoss with all-background labels - Assert finite loss
test_bce_dice_cldice_all_background_finite(): - BceDiceClDiceLoss with all-background labels - Assert finite loss
tdd-spec.green:
Pure test file — verifying all compound losses survive all-background data. The NaN guards in VesselCompoundLoss (line 120), TopologyCompoundLoss (line 341), and _WrappedSoftclDiceLoss (line 165) should handle the clDice degeneration. CAPE and SkeletonRecallLoss have their own guards (valid_count=0 → 0.0).
If any test fails: add the same NaN guard pattern to the failing loss.

acceptance-criteria.criterion: All 5 compound losses are finite with all-background labels
No crash in backward() for any compound loss
NaN guards fire where expected (cldice degeneration)

task.title: _average_checkpoints: weights_only=True with non-standard state_dict
task.description:
post_training_flow.py:663-671 — _average_checkpoints() uses torch.load(path, weights_only=True). If a checkpoint contains custom objects beyond tensors (e.g., optimizer state with LR scheduler objects, or non-standard keys), weights_only=True will raise UnpicklingError.
The existing guard (line 658-660) catches empty list, and the key mismatch warning (lines 678-688) catches key drift. But there is NO guard for the torch.load failure itself. If ANY fold checkpoint is corrupt, _average_checkpoints crashes and the entire post-training flow fails.
Fix: Add try/except around torch.load with a clear error message.
task.root-cause: Corrupt checkpoint crashes post-training with no diagnostic info

tdd-spec.red:
Add to tests/v2/unit/orchestration/test_checkpoint_averaging.py:
test_average_checkpoints_corrupt_file_raises_clear_error(): - Create a corrupt checkpoint file (write random bytes) - Call _average_checkpoints([corrupt_path], output_path) - Assert raises RuntimeError (or ValueError) with message containing the corrupt file path and "corrupt" or "load failed"
test_average_checkpoints_one_corrupt_one_valid(): - Create one valid and one corrupt checkpoint - Call _average_checkpoints([valid, corrupt], output_path) - Assert raises with message identifying WHICH checkpoint is corrupt

acceptance-criteria.criterion: Corrupt checkpoint produces clear error with file path
Error message mentions "corrupt" for actionability
Valid checkpoints still average correctly

phase.name: P1 Scientific Integrity
phase.description: Ensure scientific result correctness through comprehensive edge case testing

task.title: BettiLoss gradient masking: torch.diff on detached GT labels
task.description:
BettiLoss.forward() (loss_functions.py:265-297) computes spatial gradients via torch.diff on BOTH fg_prob (prediction) and fg_label (ground truth). fg_label is constructed from labels at line 277: fg_label = (labels_squeeze > 0).float().unsqueeze(1)
The issue: torch.diff(fg_label, dim=2) at line 288 computes gradients of a DETACHED tensor (GT labels have no grad). The gt_frag (line 292-294) is therefore a constant. The loss = |pred_frag - gt_frag| means gradient only flows through pred_frag. This is CORRECT for a loss function (GT is fixed), but:
(a) If pred_frag == gt_frag exactly (random init), gradient is zero (saddle point) (b) torch.diff reduces spatial dims by 1, so (B,1,D,H,W) → (B,1,D-1,H,W) etc. This is fine for mean() but should be tested with minimum spatial dims.
No test covers: minimum spatial dims (D=2, H=2, W=2 → torch.diff yields size 1), or the exact-match zero-gradient case.
task.root-cause: Untested edge cases in BettiLoss spatial gradient computation

tdd-spec.red:
Create tests/v2/unit/losses/test_betti_loss_gradient_edge_cases.py:
test_betti_loss_minimum_spatial_dims(): - logits = torch.randn(1, 2, 2, 2, 2, requires_grad=True) # D=H=W=2 - labels = torch.randint(0, 2, (1, 1, 2, 2, 2)).float() - loss_fn = BettiLoss() - loss = loss_fn(logits, labels) - Assert torch.isfinite(loss) - loss.backward() - Assert logits.grad is not None
test_betti_loss_gradient_direction(): - logits that produce fragmented prediction (alternating 0/1) - labels = smooth (all 1s) - loss should be positive (pred more fragmented than GT) - Verify gradient pushes prediction toward less fragmentation
test_betti_loss_all_zero_predictions(): - logits = torch.full((1, 2, 8, 8, 8), -10.0, requires_grad=True) # softmax → near 0 foreground - labels = torch.ones(1, 1, 8, 8, 8) # all foreground - loss = BettiLoss()(logits, labels) - Assert torch.isfinite(loss) - loss.backward() - Assert torch.isfinite(logits.grad).all()
test_betti_loss_perfect_match_near_zero(): - Create logits that closely match labels (pred_frag ≈ gt_frag) - Assert loss is near zero (but not NaN)
tdd-spec.green: Pure test file — verifying BettiLoss (loss_functions.py:240-297) handles edge cases correctly. The implementation uses torch.diff + abs().mean() which should be numerically stable. These tests guard against future changes that might break the spatial gradient computation.

acceptance-criteria.criterion: BettiLoss works with minimum spatial dims (2x2x2)
All-zero prediction produces finite loss and gradient
Perfect prediction match produces near-zero loss

task.title: SkeletonRecallLoss GPU memory: skeletonize() moves to CPU per-batch
task.description:
skeleton_recall.py:79-118 — _skeletonize_gt() moves each batch element to CPU (.detach().cpu().numpy() at line 101), computes skimage.skeletonize(), then moves back to GPU (.to(device=device) at line 117). For large batches or volumes, this CPU-GPU transfer could be slow. More critically:
The RETURNED skeleton is .detach() (line 118), which is correct. But no test verifies that the skeleton tensor does NOT accumulate in GPU memory across iterations (memory leak potential if PyTorch retains the computation graph through the skeleton).
Fix: Add a test that verifies skeleton is truly detached and does not accumulate GPU memory across multiple forward passes.
task.root-cause: Potential GPU memory accumulation from CPU-GPU transfer pattern

tdd-spec.red:
Create tests/v2/unit/losses/test_skeleton_recall_memory.py:
test_skeleton_recall_detached_skeleton(): - loss_fn = SkeletonRecallLoss() - logits, labels = make_test_pair() - loss = loss_fn(logits, labels) - Access loss_fn internal — check the skeleton tensor is detached - Actually: verify by calling forward twice and checking that gradient graph does not grow (gc.collect between calls)
test_skeleton_recall_multiple_forward_no_leak(): - loss_fn = SkeletonRecallLoss() - Run forward 10 times - After each: loss.backward(), optimizer.zero_grad() - Verify no Python-level memory growth (check gc object count or simply verify no OOM on small test data)
test_skeleton_recall_batch_size_scaling(): - Verify batch_size=1 and batch_size=4 both work - Assert loss scales reasonably (not NaN for larger batch)
tdd-spec.green: Pure test file — verifying the CPU-GPU transfer pattern in skeleton_recall.py:79-118 does not leak memory. The .detach() at line 118 should prevent graph accumulation. These tests verify that invariant.

acceptance-criteria.criterion: Skeleton tensor is detached (no gradient graph accumulation)
Multiple forward passes do not leak memory
Batch size 1 and 4 both produce finite results

task.title: BettiMatchingLoss silent gudhi fallback: test proxy vs real behavior
task.description:
betti_matching.py:30-39 — BettiMatchingLoss checks gudhi availability at import time. If gudhi is not installed, it silently uses a "differentiable proxy" that is NOT the actual Betti matching from Stucki ICML 2023. The warning at line 36 fires at import time but is easily missed in a batch of 60+ SkyPilot jobs.
In our environment, gudhi IS installed (via uv sync --all-extras). But if a Docker image is built without it, the proxy kicks in silently. No test verifies that the gudhi-backed path and the proxy path produce DIFFERENT results, which is important for scientific reproducibility — the proxy is an approximation.
Fix: Add tests that verify both paths and document the behavioral difference.
task.root-cause: Silent fallback from exact topology matching to approximate proxy

tdd-spec.red:
Add to tests/v2/unit/test_betti_matching_loss.py:
test_betti_matching_gudhi_available(): - import gudhi — assert it is importable - Verify betti_matching._GUDHI_AVAILABLE is True - This guards against Docker images missing gudhi
test_betti_matching_differentiable(): - logits = torch.randn(1, 2, 16, 16, 8, requires_grad=True) - labels = torch.randint(0, 2, (1, 1, 16, 16, 8)).float() - loss_fn = BettiMatchingLoss() - loss = loss_fn(logits, labels) - Assert torch.isfinite(loss) - loss.backward() - Assert logits.grad is not None
test_betti_matching_all_background_finite(): - labels = torch.zeros(1, 1, 16, 16, 8) - Assert loss is finite (should be 0 or near-0)
test_betti_matching_proxy_warns_when_no_gudhi(monkeypatch): - monkeypatch.setattr("minivess.pipeline.vendored_losses.betti_matching._GUDHI_AVAILABLE", False) - Instantiate BettiMatchingLoss with caplog - Assert warning about "differentiable proxy" in caplog
tdd-spec.green: Pure test file — verifying BettiMatchingLoss behavior in both gudhi-available and proxy-fallback modes. If gudhi is NOT installed in the test environment, the gudhi_available test will fail — this is intentional (it catches Docker image misconfiguration).
tdd-spec.verify: uv run pytest tests/v2/unit/test_betti_matching_loss.py -v make test-staging

acceptance-criteria.criterion: gudhi is importable in the test environment
BettiMatchingLoss is differentiable with gudhi backend
All-background labels produce finite loss
Proxy fallback logs a warning (not silent)

task.title: Cross-flow EXPERIMENT_NAME consistency between train and analysis
task.description:
train_flow.py:1660 reads EXPERIMENT_NAME env var (default "minivess_training"). analysis_flow.py reads UPSTREAM_EXPERIMENT env var to find training runs. post_training_flow.py reads EXPERIMENT env var at line 1598.
These three flows must agree on the experiment name for the cross-flow contract to work. If train_flow uses "debug_dynunet_cbdice_cldice" but analysis_flow searches for "minivess_training", zero runs are found and analysis is skipped.
No test verifies this cross-flow consistency. The SkyPilot YAML env vars are the source of truth, but a test should verify the DEFAULT values match.
task.root-cause: Cross-flow experiment name mismatch causes silent no-op analysis

tdd-spec.red:
Create tests/v2/unit/orchestration/test_cross_flow_experiment_name.py:
test_train_flow_experiment_name_env_var(): - Read train_flow.py source with ast.parse - Find the argparse add_argument for --experiment-name - Extract the default value - Assert it reads from EXPERIMENT_NAME env var
test_analysis_flow_reads_upstream_experiment(): - Read analysis_flow.py source with ast.parse - Find os.environ.get("UPSTREAM_EXPERIMENT") usage - Assert it exists (the flow must read from env, not hardcode)
test_skypilot_yaml_experiment_names_consistent(): - Read deployment/skypilot/train_factorial.yaml - Extract EXPERIMENT_NAME from env vars - Read deployment/skypilot/analysis.yaml (if exists) - Extract UPSTREAM_EXPERIMENT from env vars - Assert the naming pattern is consistent (e.g., both use the same EXPERIMENT variable or one derives from the other)
test_default_experiment_name_documented(): - The default "minivess_training" appears in train_flow.py - Assert this string appears in at least one config or doc
tdd-spec.green: Pure test file — verifying cross-flow experiment name consistency. Use yaml.safe_load for YAML files, ast.parse for Python source. Follow pattern from test_docker_image_consistency.py.

acceptance-criteria.criterion: Train flow reads EXPERIMENT_NAME from env
Analysis flow reads UPSTREAM_EXPERIMENT from env
SkyPilot YAML env vars are consistent across flows

task.title: ExperimentTracker: test log_hydra_config and log_artifact error paths
task.description:
tracking.py:498-555 — log_hydra_config() and tracking.py:344-346 — log_artifact() are called during training to persist the resolved config and profiling data. If MLflow is unreachable at these points (different from the resume check — this is DURING training), these methods could either crash the training run or silently lose the config artifact.
No test covers: (a) log_hydra_config with no active MLflow run, (b) log_artifact with a non-existent file path, (c) log_artifact when MLflow is unreachable mid-training.
These are P1 because: losing the config artifact makes the run non-reproducible, but it should NOT crash training. The fix is to ensure these methods are fault-tolerant (log warning, continue).
task.root-cause: Config artifact loss during MLflow outage makes runs non-reproducible

tdd-spec.red:
Create tests/v2/unit/observability/test_tracker_error_paths.py:
test_log_artifact_nonexistent_file_warns(): - tracker = ExperimentTracker(config, tracking_uri=str(tmp_path / "mlruns")) - tracker.start_run() - tracker.log_artifact(Path("/nonexistent/file.txt")) - Assert warning logged (not crash)
test_log_hydra_config_no_active_run(): - tracker = ExperimentTracker(config, tracking_uri=str(tmp_path / "mlruns")) - Do NOT call start_run() - tracker.log_hydra_config(resolved_config={}) - Assert it handles gracefully (warning or no-op, not crash)
test_log_artifact_valid_file(): - Create a valid file in tmp_path - tracker.start_run() - tracker.log_artifact(valid_file) - Assert no error
test_tracker_start_run_creates_experiment(): - tracker = ExperimentTracker(config, tracking_uri=str(tmp_path / "mlruns")) - tracker.start_run() - Assert MLflow experiment was created - tracker.end_run()
tdd-spec.green: Pure test file — verifying ExperimentTracker error paths. The implementation at tracking.py should already handle most of these via MLflow's internal error handling. If any test reveals a crash, add try/except with logger.warning in the affected method.

acceptance-criteria.criterion: Non-existent file does not crash log_artifact
No active run does not crash log_hydra_config
Valid file logging works correctly
All error paths produce warnings (not silent failures)

verification.gate: uv run ruff check src/ tests/
